strengths: Hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and frameworks supporting agentic workflows at re:cinq, directly aligning with the multi-agent architecture requirements.

Proven capability in designing highly scalable, low-latency microservices and event-driven systems, demonstrated by handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby and architecting the GISCollective platform.

Strong engineering craftsmanship and relevant fintech exposure (RIDE Capital), evidenced by driving TDD, CI/CD implementations, and organizing technical workshops, which aligns perfectly with the need for a reliability-focused engineering culture.
concerns: Lacks explicit executive-level (CTO) leadership titles and a demonstrated track record of scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people.
AI experience appears relatively recent and focused on developer tooling, lacking explicit evidence of operating AI systems in regulated, high-stakes financial environments.
No direct mention of managing outsourced engineering partners or architecting specific algorithmic working-capital and credit-risk models.
response.justification: Bogdan presents a compelling technical foundation for this role, blending highly relevant recent experience in AI-agent infrastructure with deep, battle-tested expertise in low-latency, high-throughput microservices and event-driven architectures. While his profile leans more toward a highly senior technical leader and founder than a traditional executive CTO who has scaled large teams, his background in fintech, relentless focus on engineering craftsmanship, and proven ability to architect complex distributed systems make him a strong technical fit capable of designing the robust, real-time AI trading infrastructure the role demands.
